rulings.Case Title: ANDREW THOMPSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Requests For Admission And To Strike Objections Thereto
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC¿s motion to compel plaintiff to respond to requests for admission and to strike is granted. All plaintiff¿s objections are stricken and plaintiff is required to provide non-evasive responses to the requests within fifteen days. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And C
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of the person most qualified and custodian of records of the Boeing Co. was continued from January 6 per the parties¿ agreement. If all issues are not resolved, by 4:30pm today, the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on January 20 at 9:00am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary And Nonmonetary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. § 2030.300(E), C.C.P. § 2023.030 (A) And (D)(2) Et Seq., C.C.P. § 2031.310(E), And C.C.P. § 2031.320(C)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion for monetary and nonmonetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. The request for monetary sanction of $25,000 per day until defendant answers the post-judgment interrogatories is denied on the grounds that there is no California authority authorizing such sanctions. The request for ¿disentitlement¿ sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is precluded from submitting any opposition to any motion seeking the turnover of personal and/or real property owed by it to this court. Such a disentitlement sanction is within the authority of this court and is reasonably necessary to prevent undue prejudice to plaintiffs from defendant¿s failure to comply with the November 23 discovery order. The request for disentitlement sanctions with regard to the turning over of property to a Pennsylvania court is denied on the grounds that there is no California authority authorizing such sanctions and the request would be an improper interference with the rules and proceeding of a court located in another state. = (220/HEK)
